title: Recombinant protein expression and purification of codon-optimized Bst-LF polymerase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8qm5s","text":"This protocol has been optimized for the recombinant expression of a codon-optimized Bst-LF polymerase.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c100b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4adau","text":"The goal of this protocol was to eliminate the use of large volumes for dyalisis through the use of concentrators for buffer exchange before storage conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"23efj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"58nke","text":"The plasmid encoding the codon-optimized Bst-LF enzyme used here can be found on reclone.org","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Transform 100 ng of pET15b plasmid containing codon-optimized Bst-LF polymerase into E. coli C41 competent cells using either heat shock or electroporation.
Spread transformed cells in LB Agar plates supplemented with 0.1 mg/mL Amp. Grow plate overnight at 37 °C .
Select a single colony from the LB agar plate to prepare a preinoculum in 10 mL LB media supplemented with 0.1 mg/mL Amp. Grow overnight at 250 rpm, 37 °C, .

Use the full volume of the preinoculum to inoculate 1 L of LB media supplemented with 0.1 mg/mL Amp (1% inoculation). Grow at 200 rpm, 37 °C, until reaching an optical density at 600 nm (OD 600 ) = 0.8.
Upon reaching OD 600 = 0.8, add IPTG to a final concentration of 0.5 millimolar (mM) and incubate overnight at 200 rpm, 18 °C, .
Centrifuge the cell culture 4000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .Then, resuspend the cell pellet in 40 mL of Buffer A freshly supplemented with 1.0 millimolar (mM) PMSF and 0.2 mg/mL lysozyme.
Sonicate on ice for 00:08:00 using cycles of 00:00:01 ON and 00:00:04 OFF at 40% amplitude (Qsonica Q125, 125W).

On an ultracentrifugation tube, incubate the unclarified lysate at 65 °C for 00:25:00 to precipitate most of E. coli proteins, and then place on ice for 00:05:00 . Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes and collect the supernatant. You might want to collect a small sample for SDS-PAGE afterwards.

On a 5 mL HisTrap column (GE Healthcare) pre-equilibrated with 10 column volumes (c.v.) (here, 50 mL) of Buffer A , load the supernant. Wash with 10-20 c.v. of Buffer B . Then, elute with 5 c.v. of Buffer C , collecting the eluted fractions every 1 mL in 1.5 ml tubes.

To quickly pool the fractions containing the protein of interest, prepare a 96-well plate or 1.5 mL tubes with 40 µL of 5X Bradford reagent and 160 µL of distilled water. Then, add 10 µL of each protein fraction and compare against a blank reference sample corresponding to 10 µL of Buffer B . You can determine your protein-containing fractions either by absorbance at 595 nm on a plate reader or visually by comparing the blue coloration of each fraction against the blank reference. Pool your fractions and collect a 10 µL sample for SDS-PAGE.

To decrease the imidazole concentration, perform a buffer exchange step with an Amicon Ultra-15 concentrator (Merck Millipore). Centrifuge 3000 x g, 10 °C, 10 minutes , discard the flowthrough, add Buffer D to decrease the imidazole concentration and repeat this step, until the imidazole concentration reaches < 30 mM.

Recover the concentrated protein and determine its concentration using the Bradford assay. Then, supplement with 0.2 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 0.2 % volume Triton X-100, 2 millimolar (mM) DTT and add glycerol up to 50 % volume to reach Storage Conditions . Do consider that a final protein concentration ≤ 2 mg/mL is appropriate for subsequent experiments.
Generate 200 µL aliquots of the enzyme and store it at -20 °C until required.
Incubate the resuspended cells 80 rpm, Room temperature, 30 minutes .
SDS-PAGE at 10% PA of eluted fractions from IMAC purification of Bst-LF. The high MW contaminant is due to disulfide bond formation and eliminated upon addition of DTT (data not shown).
creator: Cesar A Ramirez-Sarmiento
